title: Gas pipe security device
abstract: A gas pipe security system comprises a bung device arranged so as to be placed inside the bore of a pipe in use and constructed. When gas passes through the pipe and bung the bung generates a sensing signal, and a sensor device detects the sensing signal generated by the bung and provides an alarm signal based thereon..
claims: Claims 1. A gas pipe security system comprising a bung device arranged so as to be placed inside the bore of a pipe in use and constructed such that, when gas passes through the pipe and bung the bung generates a sensing signal, and a sensor device for detecting the sensing signal generated by the bung and providing an alarm signal based thereon. 2. A system according to claim 1 , wherein the sensing signal is an acoustic signal. 3. A system according to claim 1 , wherein the sensor device detects the frequency of the acoustic signal. 4. A system according to claim 1 or 2 in which the bung is arranged with a flow restriction that generates a whistling effect as the acoustic signal. 5. A system according to claim 1 or 2 wherein the bung device comprises a rotor mechanism which is driven by gas passing through the bung in use and an acoustic signal generator driven by mechanical connection to the rotor. 6. A system according to claim 1 , wherein the sensing signal is an electromagnetic signal. 7. A system according to any preceding claim, wherein the sensor device is placed, in use, outside the pipe. 8. A system according to any preceding claim wherein the sensor device has a self-contained power supply and transmits the alarm signal wirelessly so that it is a stand-alone unit. 9. A system according to any preceding claim in which the sensor device is further configured to generate an alarm signal when it is removed from the pipe in use. 10. A system according to any preceding claim wherein the sensor device is further configured to provide updates related to its status to a remote receiver on a predetermined or request basis. 1 1. A system according to any preceding claim when the bung further comprises a threaded element on one of its surfaces for engagement with an insertion and/or removal tool.
